sourceMovie.title: Love Don't Co$t a Thing
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Love Don't Co$t a Thing (2003) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Greenberg
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Love Don't Co$t a Thing clicked for you, Greenberg (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Love Don't Co$t a Thing?
faq.answer: Try Greenberg, Cyrus, The Clique—each shares DNA with Love Don't Co$t a Thing while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Love Don't Co$t a Thing?
faq.answer: Love Don't Co$t a Thing (2003) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Family, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Love Don't Co$t a Thing worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
